records.summary: A post-procedure chest X-ray (PA view) displayed on a monitor, showing the thoracic cavity with visible lung fields, heart silhouette, and bony thorax. Patient information and timestamp are visible on the image footer.

records.stage_evidence: Radiology report describing findings from a chest X-ray

ocr.verbatim:
PATIENT: AGE/SEX:55 Y /F
REF BY: DATE: 24/03/2026
CHEST X-RAY (PA VIEW)
> Prominent Hilar shadows on right side? Vascular
> Non homogenous opacity seen in lower zone of right lung ?Pneumonitis
> Cardiothoracic ratio is ~17/27 ...likely cardiomegaly.
> Flattening of bilateral hemidiaphragm ?COPD
> Bony cage appears normal.
> Costo-phrenic angles are bilaterally clear.
> Trachea is central in position.

IMPRESSION:-
• ?COPD
• RIGHT LOWER ZONE PNEUMONITIS
• BORDERLINE CARDIOMEGALY

Adv: Clinical Correlation

JR JR ASSISTANT PROFESSOR ASSISTANT PROFESSOR ASSISTANT PROFESSOR
This is a professional opinion not a diagnosis. NOT MEANT FOR MEDICOLEGAL PURPOSES

IGH
PROFESSOR & H.O.D.

records.summary: This is a typed chest X-ray report (PA view) for a 55-year-old female patient dated 24/03/2026. The report lists findings including prominent hilar shadows, non-homogenous opacity in the right lower lung zone, a cardiothoracic ratio suggestive of borderline cardiomegaly, flattening of hemidiaphragms, and normal bony cage. The impression includes possible COPD, right lower zone pneumonitis, and borderline cardiomegaly, with a note advising clinical correlation.
key_observations: Findings: Prominent hilar shadows on right side, non-homogenous opacity in right lower lung zone, cardiothoracic ratio ~17/27 suggesting cardiomegaly, flattening of hemidiaphragms, normal bony cage, clear costophrenic angles, central trachea
Impression: ?COPD, Right lower zone pneumonitis, Borderline cardiomegaly
Disclaimer: This is a professional opinion not a diagnosis. NOT MEANT FOR MEDICOLEGAL PURPOSES
